metadata.description: Ideology definitions for Nation Builder educational game

liberal.name: Liberalism
liberal.description: Emphasizes individual freedom, market economics, democratic governance, and limited government intervention.
coreValues: Rule of law and constitutional government
liberal.successPattern: Thrives with high economic growth, strong democratic institutions, cultural diversity, and individual mobility

conservative.name: Conservatism
conservative.description: Values tradition, stability, established institutions, and gradual rather than radical change.
conservative.successPattern: Excels with stable institutions, low crime, strong rule of law, and cultural continuity

socialist.name: Socialism
socialist.description: Prioritizes economic equality, collective ownership, and strong government role in ensuring social welfare.
socialist.successPattern: Succeeds with low inequality, universal services, strong worker rights, and social cohesion

ecological.name: Ecologism
ecological.description: Centers environmental sustainability, ecological balance, and long-term planetary health in all policy decisions.
ecological.successPattern: Thrives with clean environment, renewable energy, sustainable practices, and resource conservation

authoritarian.name: Authoritarianism
authoritarian.description: Emphasizes strong centralized leadership, order over freedom, and concentrated state power for stability and control.
coreValues: Order and stability over individual freedom
authoritarian.successPattern: Effective with strong institutions, low crime, high security, and efficient governance

feminist.name: Feminism
feminist.description: Focuses on gender equality, social justice, and dismantling patriarchal structures across all areas of society.
feminist.successPattern: Succeeds with gender equality, inclusive policies, care work support, and diverse representation

protectionism.description: Protective policies appear across ideologies but serve different purposes

variants.economic: Liberal and conservative protectionism for domestic industries
variants.cultural: Conservative and authoritarian protection of traditional values
variants.social: Socialist and feminist protection of vulnerable groups

populism.description: Appeal to 'the people' manifests differently across ideologies

variants.left: Socialist populism focusing on economic inequality

educationalNotes.purpose: These definitions help students understand how different ideological frameworks shape policy priorities and governance approaches
educationalNotes.usage: The game tracks alignment with these ideologies based on policy choices, not explicit ideological labels
educationalNotes.realWorld: Real political systems often blend elements from multiple ideologies rather than following pure ideological lines
